title: D1-004d1 admission ledger core
description:
## Goal and why
D1-004d1 admission ledger core: add the durable Postgres admission ledger foundation — Drizzle migration/schema, admissionLedger.ts, session advisory fence, and CLI destructive-diff read — keyed by host, binding, and execution identity. Commit before effects, use advisory fencing, and fail closed on mismatch or commit failure. This is the reusable admission ledger core; it does not own consumer wiring or rollback journaling.

## Scope
One reviewable PR. ≤400 net lines. Preserve Decisions 21–24, workspace-first authority, and no pure mode. Scope is narrowed to the ledger core only: Drizzle migration/schema + admissionLedger.ts + session advisory fence + CLI destructive-diff read. D1-004d2 owns Pi first-effect admission consumers; D1-004d3 owns the WorkspaceBridge operation admission seam. D1-004e owns append-only rollback-journal creation, prepared-state reads, and recovery fencing. D1-005c is the first concrete stable-core consumer of the admission hook.

## Explicit exclusions
No rollback-journal reads or writes, publication pointer, served acknowledgement, scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor. No Pi service decorator, createAgent.ts facade, slash/reload hook wiring, or WorkspaceBridge operation wiring (those are D1-004d2/d3).

## Acceptance and proof
Focused unit tests prove admission rows and advisory fencing commit before the first effect and reject a mismatch/commit failure without effects. Run targeted typecheck/tests and attach redacted output.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance field: focused proof verifies only this bead's owned contract.

Acceptance Criteria:
Focused proof verifies: admitted binding remains non-removable after process restart and revision-directory cleanup; landing-only revisions preserve execution identity; binding-id reuse with different execution facts fails closed; concurrent admission is idempotent; kill-after-commit-before-effect leaves a deliberate permanent fence; and pre-commit/lock failure produces no effect. No rollback journal or stable-core publication behavior is implemented.

Owned stable codes: D1_ADMISSION_IDENTITY_MISMATCH, D1_ADMISSION_RECORD_FAILED (must match these names exactly — do not invent variants).

Split per sizing audit 2026-07-13; siblings D1-004d2/d3 carry the consumer seams (pre-declared in PR-PLAN).

description:

title: M3: Seneca static catalog, selector, and two-agent deployment proof
description:
## Goal / Why
Prove Seneca as the first normal deployment consumer of the reusable static catalog: compile two named agents, render a safe selector, and run both in one authorized workspace on one Vercel persistent sandbox.

## Exact package/repo/file seams
Seneca `agents/<name>/*`, `scripts/compile-agents.mts`, `scripts/build-agent-catalog.mts`, `scripts/build-app.mts`, `Dockerfile`, `src/server/agents/{catalog,declarations}.ts`, `src/server/{main,dev}.ts`, `src/front/SenecaAgentSelector.tsx`, `src/front/main.tsx`, `src/server/__tests__/agents/**`, and `e2e/**`; confirm names against current Seneca main.

## Exclusions
No private parallel composer, Core DB/CAS, dynamic catalog, per-agent hostname, fleet/controller/Seneca Cloud, custom-tool runner, or PR/deployment state action beyond the normal proof environment.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Build compiles at least `dummy` and `reviewer`, rejects duplicate/invalid bundles, and emits deterministic immutable descriptor/binding/catalog evidence.
- Authorized members receive only server-issued `{id,label,routeBase}` views; nonmembers and foreign/unknown targets fail before resolution; selector uses those route bases.
- Two named agents in workspace W acquire one Vercel persistent sandbox name/handle/root; one writes a sentinel that the other reads, while W2 has a distinct handle and cannot read W.
- Prompts, tool catalogs, sessions, namespaces, and provenance remain distinct; removed/mismatched catalog history reads but effects fail closed.
- Normal image deployment changes new-session identity only and introduces no hostname-per-agent, Core DB/CAS, controller, or Cloud behavior.

## Proof commands/manual demo
Run Seneca typecheck, focused unit/integration tests, e2e, normal image build, authenticated two-agent smoke, Vercel provider-handle instrumentation, sentinel cross-agent/manual demo, W2/nonmember/foreign negatives, and historical read/effect-denial checks. description:

Full-app must retain one byte-compatible `primary` behavior.

## Exclusions
No Core DB/migration/store/binding/default/CAS/event tables; no mutable registry, install/update API, marketplace, runtime upload, hostname/fleet/controller/Seneca Cloud work; no browser trusted identity/handle/root/digest input; no custom-tool host imports; no source, branch, or history deletion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Browser uses server-issued route bases only and keeps selected Agent API base separate from Workspace/Core APIs; Workspace front/shared has no Agent value imports and dispatch remains `UiBridge.postCommand`.
- Full-app supplies exactly `primary`, `routeBase: ''`, no selector, no CAS code/migration, and preserves legacy routes/UI/session behavior.

Run focused Core, Workspace, Agent, and full-app tests/typechecks/builds plus import-boundary audits. Add a package-level tracer proving `{W,primary}` and `{W,reviewer}` share one fake/provider handle/filesystem, sentinel write/read succeeds across them, W2 is isolated, prompts/tools/namespaces/headers are separate, nonmember/foreign/unknown-ID requests fail before resolution, safe views are exact, prefixed paths work, removed history reads while effects reject, and full-app primary compatibility stays green.

## Rollback / abort
Keep existing single-primary composition. Reject unknown/mismatched descriptors and identity before effects; do not add fallback/default mutation or rewrite legacy sessions. Abort the PR if synchronized-main API review finds the declared seams unavailable rather than introducing a parallel store/registry.

## Review budget
High package/API boundary review; one coherent reusable abstraction PR with Core authorization, Workspace server/front, Agent provenance, and full-app compatibility reviewers.

title: SEC-1: prompt-injection & data-exfiltration review for vertical-agent document ingestion
description:
The real v1 attack surface has no bead. Adversarial review and hardening of the path where an agent ingests untrusted third-party documents — the accounting agent's invoice intake (per DEMAND-1's accounting vertical) is the canonical case, but this applies to any vertical agent's document-ingestion path. Two threats in scope: (1) prompt injection — malicious document content (invoice/PDF/email body) driving unintended tool calls; (2) data exfiltration — injected content causing leakage of other-workspace data or system/secret data via tool output or generated artifacts.

This is the actual v1 incident surface (untrusted-document-driven agent action), not host-attestation (which is deferred behind gate wt-391-forward-tz4 and is a lower-priority v1-plus concern).

## Scope
Threat model for the document-ingestion -> tool-call path; a malicious-document test corpus (injection payloads embedded in invoice-like documents/PDFs/text); and the mitigations: input isolation (untrusted document content never treated as instruction), tool-permission scoping (ingestion path cannot invoke tools outside its bounded set), and output validation (generated output/artifacts checked before returning to caller or across workspace boundaries).

## Explicit exclusions
No scheduler, queue, registry/provisioner, control plane, generic policy framework, secret/raw-path output, or unrelated refactor. Not host-artifact-attestation (see wt-391-forward-tz4 gate — different, lower-priority v1-plus surface).

## Depends on
A first vertical agent existing (e.g. the accounting/invoice-intake demo agent referenced in GTM-CALLKITS.md) OR the M1 delivery path (landed, PR #650) providing a concrete ingestion surface to review against. No formal br dependency edge is added since neither is currently a single dispatchable bead in this tracker; this bead should not start until one of those surfaces is concretely buildable/built.

## Acceptance Criteria
A written threat model covering prompt-injection-via-document-content and cross-workspace/system data exfiltration; a malicious-document test corpus exercising both threats; and implemented mitigations (input isolation, tool-permission scoping, output validation) with tests proving each mitigation holds against the corpus. Stable-code, redaction, and no-widening assertions hold before merge.

title: SM: durability ID-ownership contract (BLOCKS the epic)
description:
## Goal and why
Ratify one session-ID ownership and migration contract before any SM implementation dispatch. This is the epic gate required by the owner decision dated 2026-07-15.

## Conflict
Closed planning PR #714 (branch plan/709-native-session-metadata-index, docs/issues/709/plan.md) proposes a browser-minted temporary native-shaped session ID sent on first prompt. #391 T2 wt-391-forward-la3 requires the session-only transport to use a runtime-owned ID returned after admission, while T1 wt-391-forward-pwd durably keys event/replay/approval state. Both cannot be authoritative.

## Recommended decision
RUNTIME-OWNED IDs. The runtime admits the session, owns canonical identity, and returns the canonical sessionId to the client after admission. This aligns with agent-host admission-ledger discipline: identity is runtime authority, never client authority. A client request/idempotency key may correlate admission but must not become the durable session ID.

## Scope
Write and owner-ratify the T1/T2/SM contract: admission request/response, canonical-ID publication point, retry semantics, pre-admission UI handle, authorization, alias lifetime, and transaction boundary for wrapper-to-native migration. Update or attach the contract to the three bead references without implementing the SM children.

## Required transactional migration
Any wrapper -> native canonical-ID migration must atomically migrate or alias every T1 durable record keyed by the old ID: stream/event rows, consumer offsets, request/idempotency rows, pending approval/input rows, and any session-scoped queue/replay cursor. Publication of the new ID and alias happens in the same transaction. Crash before commit exposes only old identity; crash after commit exposes only canonical identity plus the accepted alias. No split-brain stream, orphan offset, duplicated approval, or replay gap.

## Explicit exclusions
No browser-minted durable ID; no best-effort row copying; no delete-and-recreate stream; no client-authored workspace/user scope; no automatic mid-flight execution resurrection; no SM feature implementation; no widening T1/T2 into scheduler/registry/control-plane work.

## Acceptance Criteria
Owner-approved contract explicitly chooses runtime-owned IDs and cross-references wt-391-forward-pwd and wt-391-forward-la3. Contract inventory names every ID-keyed T1 table/store and every SM consumer. Conformance proof covers admission response loss/retry, duplicate request keys, unauthorized claimed IDs, migration at every crash boundary, offsets/replay continuity, idempotency continuity, one pending approval/input, and alias expiry only after all consumers canonicalize. The epic children remain blocked until this bead closes.

## Risks / owner blockers
Owner ratification is required. T1/T2 are deferred today; this SM epic is their named durability consumer but must not silently change their contracts. PR #714 is CLOSED and reference-only; its browser-ID rule is rejected unless the owner reverses this gate decision.

LANDED #266: packages/agent/src/front/chat/session/usePiSessions.ts restart/network retry. LANDED #351: CLI uses standalone Pi shared directory.

## Scope
List/load/search materialized native sessions from the configured cwd/session directory, enrich narrowly from private metadata, canonicalize accepted legacy aliases at the API boundary, and expose explicit pagination/caps and stable missing/forbidden/conflict codes.

## Explicit exclusions
No wrapper JSONL in results for native mode; no unbounded filesystem scan/result; no trusting client workspace fields; no adoption of header/path conflicts; no cold-starting Pi handles to list/search; no claim that persisted standalone Pi work is live.

## Acceptance Criteria
Boring and real pi --resume enumerate the same admitted native sessions once. Authorization happens before metadata/transcript disclosure. Search is bounded with an explicit tested inventory window and return cap (preserve #659s 100/20 defaults unless deliberately revised). Aliases resolve on input but responses/writes emit canonical runtime IDs. Symlink/path/header conflicts fail closed. Session-list fetches recover across restart/network failures without permanent loading. Large inventory, malformed tail, standalone-created, cross-workspace, pagination, and no-cold-start tests pass.

title: SM: native rename and manage_sessions service
description:

No loopback HTTP from the tool; no UI bridge persistence; no wrapper title writes; no transcript rewrite; no deleting executing session; no implicit delete or delete without confirm:true; no tool-supplied workspace/user authority.

## Acceptance Criteria

Preserve #615 fixes: bounded-memory backward chunk scan reaches the header/latest name when /name is followed by more than 64 KiB, works direct and migrated/aliased, tolerates malformed tails, and concurrent append cannot corrupt JSONL. Title normalization occurs before handoff. manage_sessions rename defaults only to executing canonical ID; explicit IDs authorize. Delete requires explicit ID+confirm:true, rejects self-delete, safely cleans live state, and follows replacement/retention semantics. title: SM: crash-safe legacy wrapper migration and contraction
description:
## Goal and why
Migrate historical Pi-visible Boring wrappers to canonical native IDs/private metadata without duplicate pi --resume entries, data loss, replay gaps, or premature compatibility deletion.

## Reference implementation to recut
CLOSED #714, branch plan/709-native-session-metadata-index: docs/issues/709/plan.md migration journal, alias inventory, path validation, local/hosted fences, expand-migrate-contract order. CLOSED #615, branch feat/609-native-session-rename: packages/agent/src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/sessions.ts and tests are the wrapper reader/title behavior to inventory, not architecture to merge. LANDED #234: sessions.ts repair-on-read atomic compaction proves legacy user data must survive cleanup.

## Scope
Journaled reversible migration for wrapper+native, native-missing, wrapper-only pending, conflict, retry, and crash cases. Validate and move wrappers out of Pi scanning, publish canonical alias, migrate all durable consumers, retain collision-safe backups, then contract legacy writes before reads only after telemetry/retention/owner approval.

## Explicit exclusions
No automatic user-data deletion; no invented native transcript/ID for wrapper-only state; no shared-multi-host migration using POSIX locks; no path-hint trust; no reader contraction before local+hosted fence; no best-effort T1 row copy.

## Acceptance Criteria
Before every read/move: containment, realpath/lstat, regular-file/no-symlink, no hardlink surprise, trusted native header ID, and content hash validation. Local migration uses one owner-token lock and refuses multi-host mode. Every journal phase is idempotent/recoverable; backups never overwrite different content. In the same transaction as canonical alias publication, migrate T1 stream/event rows, offsets/cursors, idempotency/request rows, approval/pending-input rows, queues, and every audited session-keyed store. Crash injection proves old-only before commit and canonical+alias after; no duplicate approval, prompt, event, or replay gap. Real copied-data pi --resume shows one session. Legacy reads remain until explicit retention/rollback approval.

## Risks / owner blockers
Blocked by the durability contract and local foundations. Hosted execution additionally waits for the hosted metadata/lease adapter. Legacy reader deletion is owner-approved contraction, never bundled into initial migration.

title: SM: authorized task-session bindings and CLI store
description:
## Goal and why
Give Tasks a DB-neutral, workspace-scoped task-to-canonical-session binding contract plus durable CLI file adapter and exact authenticated routes.

## Scope
list/link/unlink, create-and-link, link-existing via shared search, reopen, unavailable-session unlink, CLI persistence at the host-owned workspace/task location, and host-composed session authorization port. Bind canonical IDs only after admission.

## Explicit exclusions
No task title as identity; title/displayRef is presentation only. No client-provided workspace identity. No plugin loopback auth replay. No silent unbound chat open. No implicit orphan deletion. No filesystem lock claim for multi-host hosted mode. No activity polling in this bead.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tuple (workspaceId, adapterId, taskId, sessionId) is unique; link is idempotent. Every selected sessionId is authorized/canonicalized through the shared service before persistence. File store serializes per store, uses exclusive lock plus atomic temp+rename/fsync, survives restart, and passes shared concurrent link and link+unlink conformance. Create-and-link orders runtime admission -> canonical ID -> authorized binding -> open; response/failure is deterministic and exposes a created orphan for retry rather than deleting or opening silently. Caller scope is host-derived. Route, store, restart, standalone Pi link, and cross-workspace tests pass.

## Risks / owner blockers
Blocked by gate, identity, list/search, and session management. The old branch is reference-only. Hosted persistence is a separate child consuming the same conformance suite.

title: SM: hosted metadata and Postgres binding adapters
description:
## Goal and why
Inject host-owned durable session metadata/migration authority and a core-owned Postgres task-session binding adapter for hosted multi-runtime deployments.

## Reference implementation to recut
CLOSED #696, branch feat/614-hosted-task-session-bindings: packages/core/drizzle/0018_task_session_bindings.sql; packages/core/src/server/db/schema.ts; server/db/stores/PostgresTaskSessionBindingStore.ts and tests; createCoreWorkspaceAgentServer.ts and hosted integration tests; packages/workspace/src/app/server/createWorkspaceAgentServer.ts; Tasks conformance/routes. LANDED #386/#339 and #540: /data/pi-sessions durable sibling root and mounted-volume ownership.

## Scope
Core-owned Postgres binding migration/store; host-injected private metadata and migration transaction adapter; authenticated workspace/user scope resolver; multi-host lease/fencing; runtime replacement/restart behavior; hosted capability fence. Tasks and agent packages remain DB-neutral.

## Explicit exclusions
No local metadata file as hosted auth; no POSIX lock pretend-CAS across hosts; no plugin-owned DB import; no DB service embedded in sandbox; no hosted native-ID opt-in before adapter/auth proof; no claim of cross-instance live activity here.

## Acceptance Criteria
Postgres binding adapter enforces unique tuple and idempotent upsert/list/unlink and passes the exact shared conformance suite. Hosted composition actually injects both adapters. Metadata/alias publication and the durability-gate T1 row migration share one DB transaction; migration leases are fenced and crash/retry safe. Host derives workspace/user scope and proves cross-workspace denial. Restart, runtime/sandbox replacement, concurrent hosts, migration fence, and rollback are covered in real Postgres integration tests that cannot green-skip. Session roots remain host-owned durable data at BORING_AGENT_SESSION_ROOT, typically /data/pi-sessions sibling to /data/workspaces, with entrypoint ownership proof.

## Risks / owner blockers
Blocked by gate, identity/private metadata, and DB-neutral task bindings. Database migration and hosted authorization require human review before deployment.

description:

title: D1-005c collection preload and atomic publication
description:
## Goal and why
D1-005c collection preload and atomic publication: consume the D1-004d admission seam and D1-004e journal protocol to preload bounded resolved bundles, publish the durable atomic collection pointer, atomically swap one stable core, start ingress last, and record served acknowledgement. It attaches lazy first-effect admission and unused-add rollback through those completed seams.

## Scope

title: D1-005c1 bounded collection prepare and stable-core controller
description:
Goal: implement the load-bearing in-process mechanism that prepares N independently resolved agent bundles and swaps one immutable live collection inside the stable core without disrupting retained bindings.

Scope: consume N independent injected P6-R resolutions; pre-read bundle sizes before runtime creation; enforce max bindings, per-bundle bytes, total bytes, and bounded concurrent preload. Build candidate-only runtime handles, atomically replace the complete immutable collection once, emit an exact served acknowledgement {revision,digest}, dispose failed/unused candidate handles, and preserve retained binding handles/identities across N+1. No first-effect admission rows are created during resolve/preload/all-ready.

Exclusions: no filesystem prepare/commit signal, durable pointer orchestration, ingress start, root command/main production wiring, scheduler/registry/control plane, host-artifact attestation, RuntimeIsolationEvidenceV1, VerifiedD1HostExecution, approved-host-release ceremony, custom launcher, active replacement/removal.

Acceptance/proof: cap and cap+1 reject before runtime creation; per-bundle/total limits and bounded concurrency are proven; partial failure disposes only candidate handles and leaves old collection serving; one atomic swap exposes no mixed collection; exact served identity; retained request/session/reconnect handles survive N+1 byte-identical; preload creates zero admission rows/effects.

Stable codes: owns D1_COLLECTION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED exactly; reuses D1_COLLECTION_NOT_READY. Invent no variants.

Decision 25: load-bearing core only. Hard <=400 total net lines including tests/scripts. Child 1 of exactly 2 for vup.
notes: 2026-07-14 implementation dispatched after Decision 25 two-child split. Hard <=400 total net. Build only bounded preload plus stable-core atomic collection controller; no durable signal/wiring (vup.2), no attestation layer or ceremony.

All 15 checks green. Proof: ordered package build, focused controller tests, full-app typecheck, E2E, remote-worker smoke, image smoke; final 343 net under hard 400. Multi-pass review fixed runtime-input identity drift, concurrent prepare authority, cleanup retry ownership, atomic size cache, and additive-only live-handle safety.

title: D1-005c2 durable root-core publication and wiring
description:
Goal: make the D1-005c1 stable-core collection swap durable and operable through the existing root command, atomic pointer, rollback journal, and ingress lifecycle.

Scope: add one fixed root-owned prepare/commit publication signal with exact pending identity validation. Map production command effects so preload prepares the candidate, durable publication commits the pointer, and verifyActive requests one core commit then requires the exact served {revision,digest} acknowledgement. Expose bounded redacted local status. Start ingress last on first boot only after exact served ack. Connect D1-004e removal recovery/unused-add rollback. Converge safely after lost prepare/commit signal, lost ack, or core restart; retain the old collection on failed prepare. Existing lazy first-effect admission remains the sole admission seam.

Exclusions: no resolver/provider registry, scheduler/control plane, caller-triggered activation, host-artifact attestation, RuntimeIsolationEvidenceV1, VerifiedD1HostExecution, approved-host-release ceremony, d1_proof database, custom launcher, active replacement/removal, retained-session disruption.

Acceptance/proof: failed prepare leaves old collection serving; durable pointer publication occurs before exactly one swap; exact ack is required before ingress first start; no mixed collection is observable; restart/lost-signal/lost-ack recovery converges idempotently; unused unadmitted addition rolls back as a new revision while any admitted use rejects removal; status and failures expose no secret/raw path.

Stable codes: reuse D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED, D1_ROLLBACK_JOURNAL_FAILED, D1 admission codes; owns no new code.

Decision 25: load-bearing core only. Hard <=400 total net lines including tests/scripts. Child 2 of exactly 2; depends on vup.1.
notes: 2026-07-14 dispatched after vup.1/#754 merge. Implement durable root/core prepare-commit signal, exact served ack, production dependency wiring, ingress-last, and D1-004e recovery/rollback handoff only. Decision 25 core path: no RuntimeIsolationEvidenceV1, VerifiedD1HostExecution, approved-host-release/artifact/env/Caddy attestation or ceremony. Hard <=400 total net.

title: D1-005c2a core publication protocol and served lifecycle
description:
Goal: make the stable core the atomic serving authority through a fixed root-triggerable but non-authoritative Unix-socket prepare/commit/status protocol.

Scope: add canonical root-owned D1PendingPublicationV1 identity at the fixed host-state path and a bounded fixed Unix socket at /run/boring/d1-control/publication.sock. Socket payload carries only action + operationId; every prepare/commit re-reads immutable revision, pending record and durable pointer. Core owns an in-memory served-active reader used by host surface, landing, readiness and admission. Prepare validates exact expected/target/diff and preloads without changing served state. Commit requires the same pending identity and durable target pointer, performs one vup.1 atomic serve, swaps served-active and returns exact redacted status {durableRevision,servedRevision,pendingOperation}. Restart converges before public listen for expected+pending or target+pending; unrelated tuples fail closed. Extend vup.1 only for D1-004e-authorized unused rollback: exact sorted removal set, retained handles live through prepare, removed handles dispose only after target serve.

Exclusions: no root writer/client, command effects, journal mutation, ingress operation, registry/scheduler/control plane, caller activation, attestation/runtime evidence/approved-release ceremony, custom launcher, active replacement or secret/runtime rotation.

Acceptance/proof: additive prepare leaves served reader unchanged; commit exposes all N atomically; rollback retains removed handle until commit then disposes; invalid socket frame/credentials/op/digest/diff fails; bounded framing/mode; candidate cleanup; lost signal/ack retry; restart at expected and target states; request/landing/readiness/admission use only served-active authority.

Stable codes: reuse D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED only; no new code. Decision 25 core path. Hard <=400 total net. Grandchild 1 of 2 under vup.2.
notes: 2026-07-14 dispatched after nested two-part recut. Socket uses a distinct D1_CONTROL_ROOT=/run/boring/d1-control/<hostId> host bind to /run/boring/d1-control because /run/boring/d1 remains read-only; root creates the directory before core start, with root-owned parent and only app-group traversal/write for the socket leaf. The socket is untrusted wake-up only and grants no state/revision write. Acceptance requires a real full-app preloadBinding/handle adapter and the vup.1 served in-memory reader to back surface, landing, readiness and admission; fake handles or leaving routing on the durable pointer are stop-signs. Hard <=400 total net; no attestation layer.

title: D1-005c2a0 deployed-agent runtime recipe
description:
Goal: make agent routes consume the exact P6-R-selected deployment instead of treating defaultDeploymentId as routing-only metadata.

Scope: add immutable WorkspaceAgentRuntimeRecipe {workspaceId,defaultDeploymentId,resolvedDigest,instructions ref+content} and a trusted recipe resolver. Full-app adapter reads local immutable bundleRef/deploymentRef, calls the existing deployment resolver, and requires exact persisted binding identities/digests. D1 runtime scope keys include resolvedDigest and append only verified selected instructions to that binding prompt.

Exclusions: no registry/cache/remote fetch/watcher/hot reload/catalog, tool/skill/plugin selection, persistence, runtime handles/preload, pointer/socket/admission, auth decision, attestation/ceremony, new package.

Stable codes: preserve AGENT_DEFINITION_INVALID/AGENT_DEPLOYMENT_INVALID; D1 boundary reuses D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED. No new code. Decision 25 core path. Hard <=400 total net. Leaf A0.

title: D1-005c2a-pre immutable agent artifact snapshot
description:
Goal: define and materialize the minimal immutable local agent artifact contract required to turn opaque bundle/deployment refs into executable revision assets without a registry.

Scope: fixed root input slot /etc/boring/d1/agent-artifacts/<hostId>/<bindingId>/artifact.json. Refs are never path segments; the strict envelope embeds and must exactly match plan hostId/bindingId/bundleRef/deploymentRef plus existing CompiledAgentBundle and AgentDeployment schemas. Root reader enforces fixed ownership/mode, exact entries, regular singly-linked files, no symlink/traversal/change-during-read, UTF-8/JSON and collection byte/count limits. Re-resolve and compare all identities/digests, then copy canonical envelopes into <revision-temp>/agent-artifacts/<bindingId>.json before the existing whole-revision fsync+atomic rename. Revision copies are core-readable 0440 under 0710 and self-contained for rollback. Export only fixed input load and revision artifact read; no list/register/update/delete API.

Exclusions: no generic registry/cache/watcher/remote fetch/blob store/GC, approved-host-release or host-artifact/env attestation, d1_proof/release ceremony, runtime handles/routes/pointer/socket/secrets/custom launcher.

Acceptance/proof: opaque refs including bundle@1 never select paths; valid round-trip; traversal/symlink/hardlink/wrong owner/mode/extra/change/oversize/tampered definition/deployment reject before candidate rename; failed write leaves no visible revision; successful and rollback revisions read their own exact artifact; core can read but not write.

Stable codes: boundary reuses D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED; preserve internal agent definition/deployment parser codes. No new code. Decision 25 core path. Hard <=400 total net. Prerequisite for A0.

title: D1-005c2a3 Unix publication protocol and restart convergence
description:
Goal: expose served collection authority to root through a bounded untrusted Unix wake-up protocol and reconstruct exact served state after core restart.

Scope: canonical read-only-to-core D1PendingPublicationV1 at fixed root state path; fixed bounded one-request socket /run/boring/d1-control/publication.sock on distinct D1_CONTROL_ROOT bind. Payload carries only prepare/commit/status + operationId; every action rereads pending, immutable revisions and durable pointer. Prepare requires served=expected; commit requires same pending and durable=target then serves exact target. Restart before listen reconstructs no-pending durable state, expected+pending with target prepared, or target+pending served ack; unrelated tuple fails closed. Status is redacted {durableRevision,servedRevision,pendingOperation}.

Exclusions: no root client/writer, command effects, ingress, journal recovery, daemon/HTTP activation, credentials/custom launcher, attestation, second pointer. Root creates control directory in later leaf.

Acceptance/proof: bounded frame/one request and fixed modes; payload cannot select path/digest; invalid pending/path/digest rejects; prepare leaves served unchanged; commit requires durable target; lost signal/ack retries; restart convergence for all valid tuples; unrelated tuple fails; status leaks no paths/secrets; state mounts remain read-only and only control leaf writable.

Stable codes: reuse D1_PUBLICATION_FAILED; no new code. Hard <=400 total net. Leaf C, depends B.
close_reason: Consolidated into wt-391-forward-vup.2.4 (D1-005c2a3+2b Unix publication protocol, restart convergence, and root ingress-last wiring) per owner ruling 2026-07-14 — anti-fragmentation. Scope preserved verbatim in the new bead; no work lost.

title: D1-005c2b root command ingress-last and recovery wiring
description:

Scope: root atomically installs the canonical pending record, sends prepare and requires served==expected. Map effects.preload to prepare. After durable pointer publication, map verifyActive to commit and require exact durable=target, served=target, pending=operationId before writing idle. First boot compose runs migration then core-app only; exact ingress up --no-deps runs only after first served ack. Extend D1-004e activation/recovery sequence to prepared journal -> durable target pointer -> exact core commit/ack -> terminal committed. Recovery consults core status: expected/expected retries publish+commit; target/expected retries commit; target/target finalizes; unrelated tuple fails closed. Admission fences remain landed; retries idempotent; N+1 never recreates/restarts core.

Exclusions: no resolver/provider registry, scheduler/control plane, caller activation, attestation/runtime evidence/approved-release ceremony, d1_proof, custom launcher, active replacement/removal beyond unused D1-004e rollback, force-recreate or core restart.

Acceptance/proof: prepare -> pointer -> served ack order; failed prepare leaves pointer and served old; lost ack/core restart converge via status; rollback crash injection at every phase; used removal rejects; ingress cannot start before exact first ack and retry targets only exact service; N+1 does not invoke compose/core recreation; status/errors expose no paths or secrets.

title: A1: AC1-D durable dispatcher with static target resolver adapter
description:
## Goal / Why
Implement the accepted AC1-D durable in-process subagent dispatcher using a narrow static resolver adapter so native delegation resolves immutable Core-authorized declarations and Workspace composition without changing the accepted task/session/provenance contract.

## Exact package/repo/file seams
Boring Agent durable dispatcher, existing `agent.db` task-record and T1 durable event-stream seams, native in-process binding, Pi session/recovery tests, and a narrow injected `SubagentTargetResolver`; static hosts bridge Core-authorized immutable descriptors to Workspace target composition. Preserve `docs/issues/391/runtime-refactor/work/AC1-agent-consumption-contract/AC1-D-SPEC.md` accepted by closed `17q`; confirm exact source paths on synchronized main.

## Exclusions
No P6-R behavior change, parallel dispatcher/store/queue/event stream, memory/JSON/Seneca task DB substitute, scheduler, broker, generic policy framework, wire transport, contracted mode, browser-controlled root/handle/digest, custom-tool requirement, or Cloud/controller work.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Preserve accepted AgentTask v2, native in-process binding, distinct callee Pi session, 1:1 task/session mapping, existing durable narrow `agent.db` record, T1 event stream, restart recovery, `maxDepth=3`, `inputRequiredTimeoutMs=24h`, full ancestry cycle detection, originating principal, callee provenance, and accepted result/error behavior.
- The injected resolver has `resolve({principal,target})` semantics; the static adapter verifies caller workspace/principal, resolves an immutable Core-authorized target, pins execution identity, creates a distinct child session wrapper over the same workspace provider handle, and returns no browser-controlled authority.
- Existing P6-R consumers retain their adapter/behavior; no parallel dispatcher is created.
- Foreign/nonmember targets fail before task/session creation; resolution and callee failures preserve accepted stable behavior.
- Before implementation, synchronized main demonstrably provides the required `agent.db` and T1 durable seams; if absent, this bead remains blocked rather than substituting storage.

## Proof commands/manual demo
Run accepted AC1-D public-seam unit tests and durable recovery/integration coverage: A delegates to B in one workspace, separate pinned child session/same provider handle, restart/input-required/idempotency/cancel/depth/cycle/timeout cases, foreign/nonmember-before-create negatives, resolver/P6-R compatibility tests, and package typecheck. title: T1: Vercel sandbox JSON custom tool runner
description:
## Goal / Why
Add sandboxed JSON custom tools without importing user tool code into Boring, Workspace, Agent, full-app, or Seneca host processes: a Vercel-provider-visible immutable runner/artifact and a reviewed generic trusted adapter execute validated calls inside the existing workspace sandbox.

## Exact package/repo/file seams
Seneca `agents/<name>/seneca.tools.json` and contained tool build entries; normal reviewed build/catalog artifact generation; Vercel template/bake/snapshot/provisioning seams for `seneca-tool-runtime/<bundle-digest>/`; existing Workspace+Sandbox provider capability and Operations/Pi tool adapter seams; focused provider/adapter/protocol tests. Confirm exact current paths before implementation.

## Exclusions
No user custom-tool module import in host processes; no global `untrusted-tool-exec` grant to generic `/exec`, Vercel, bwrap, or worker providers; no direct-mode execution, Cloud/fleet work, marketplace/runtime upload, or claim of hostile-code isolation within one shared workspace sandbox.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A content-addressed Vercel runtime template/snapshot exposes runner, artifacts, and manifest; persistent sandboxes reconcile missing bundles additively and verify no different bytes share a digest.
- The scoped adapter advertises capability only after bundle/runner/artifact/workspace/cancellation/environment/network/resource proof; direct and generic execution modes deny it.
- Calls use CSPRNG fixed-safe IDs, one-call-per-workspace serialization, validated JSON input/output files, no model interpolation into command/path/module/environment, and post-exit validation of call ID, agent identity, resolved/artifact digest, schema, size, uniqueness, and exit class.
- Timeout, abort, missing/tampered output, digest/protocol/schema/path injection failures use stable errors; call files are removed after durable classification.
- Same-workspace visibility/tamper limitation is documented rather than described as isolation; cross-workspace, host, and secret boundaries remain enforced.

## Proof commands/manual demo
Run focused manifest/build, Vercel template/provisioning, provider capability-denial, adapter JSON protocol, CSPRNG/serialization, digest, timeout/cancel, tamper/schema/output/path-injection, and cross-workspace tests. Demonstrate one validated artifact tool call on the Vercel sandbox and direct-mode denial with recorded digest evidence.

## Rollback / abort
Keep capability absent unless every provider proof passes. Abort on runner/artifact mismatch, protocol ambiguity, or missing resource/cancellation boundary; do not fall back to host import or generic execution. Remove only additive availability from future deployments, never overwrite an existing digest.

## Review budget
High security/provider review with sandbox, tool-protocol, and host-import-boundary reviewers.

## Plan path / section
`docs/issues/391/runtime-refactor/AGENT-CLOUD-OWNERSHIP-RECUT.md` at `2b2873801`: Custom Tool JSON Subprocess Contract; T1 — Sandbox JSON custom tools; Dependency Graph.

## Owner gates
M3 normal two-agent deployment proof is required. Scoped tool capability requires hostile-tool/provider proof; any stronger isolation or user-code host import requires a separate owner decision.
